methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Summit Nanotech, a Series A climate and energy company, is evaluated against a peer set comprising one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers operating in the same sector and stage. The primary valuation anchor derives from median-based peer analysis across three same-stage, same-sector companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $150M. This reflects the midpoint of the three comparables and aligns with Summit Nanotech's current capital raised (~C$120M). The peer range spans $110M to $200M, establishing a floor and ceiling for Stage A positioning in this sector.

The triangulated valuation range supports a median estimate of $150M, with a low-end scenario of $128M and a high-end scenario of $172M. Given the single-method basis and limited sample size (three direct peers), key uncertainties include relative technology differentiation versus comparables, unit economics trajectory, and market timing within the climate-tech funding environment. The analysis is sensitive to selection of comparable companies and their respective valuation drivers. Additional quantitative anchors—such as revenue multiples or forward-looking metrics—would strengthen conviction in the derived range.
